sections.paragraph:
ρυθμός (ρυθμός, ionic ρυσμός, οῦ, ὁ,)
Etym. ρέω

I. measured motion, time, rhythm, Lat. numerus, Ar., Plat., etc.: —ἐν ρυθμῷ in time, Virgil's in numerum, Xen.; μετὰ ρυθμοῦ Thuc.; θάττονα ρυθμὸν ἐπάγειν to play in quicker time, Xen.

II. proportion or symmetry of parts, Plat.

III. generally, arrangement, order, Eur.

IV. the state or condition of the soul, temper, disposition, Theogn., etc.

V. the form or shape of a thing, Hdt.; of a breastplate, Xen.

VI. the wise, manner or fashion of a thing, Eur.; τίς ρ. φόνου; what kind of slaughter? Eur.
